Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: Development of processing strategies for multigraded selective laser melting of Ti6Al4V and IN718

Composition_Info.Note: Values from Table 3. Sum: 99.61% as reported in source

Process_Text.original: Ti6Al4V and Inconel 718 gas atomized powders (LPW, Runcorn, United Kingdom) were used throughout the work. Ti6Al4V powder size distribution was D10: 23 μm, D50: 33 μm, D90: 46 μm. Both powder feedstocks were spherical in shape. Base plates (substrates) used for the process were 12 mm thick Ti6Al4V plates for deposition of Ti6Al4V. Layer thickness was fixed at 50 μm and the focal position of the laser beam (f) was fixed on the powder bed. Laser power, scan speed and hatch distance were varied. Fixed parameters: Layer thickness z (μm): 50, Focal position f (mm): 0, Process gas: Ar. Varied parameters: Hatch distance h (μm): 50-100, Laser power P (W): 200-300-400, Scan speed v (mm/s): 500-1900, Energy density E (J/mm³): 21-320. Optimal parameters for full densification: P = 300 W, v = 700 mm/s, h = 50 μm, z = 50 μm, E = 170 J/mm³.
Process_Text.simplified: Gas atomized Ti6Al4V powder (D50: 33 μm, spherical) processed via SLM on Ti6Al4V substrate in Ar atmosphere. Fixed: layer thickness 50 μm, focal position on powder bed. Varied: hatch 50-100 μm, laser power 200-400 W, scan speed 500-1900 mm/s, energy density 21-320 J/mm³. Optimal defect-free processing: P=300 W, v=700 mm/s, h=50 μm, z=50 μm, E=170 J/mm³.

Process_Info.Equipment: Powderful SLM prototype (nLIGHT alta 1 kW single mode fiber laser, 1080 nm wavelength, beam diameter 78 μm, Smart Move scanner head, 420 mm f-theta lens, LabVIEW control, Scan Master Designer software)

Microstructure_Text.original: Optical microscopy analysis revealed that the microstructure of the components was composed of fine acicular α' grains. The α' martensitic laths are organised within prior β grain boundaries with different inclinations, mainly at ~±45° with respect to the build direction. The columnar grains are the result of epitaxial growth of the previous layer upon successive laser scans. Some lack of fusion defects could also be identified.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Fine acicular α' martensitic laths within prior β grain boundaries, oriented mainly at ±45° to build direction. Columnar grains from epitaxial growth across layers. Minor lack of fusion defects present.

Properties_Info.Note: Full densification threshold: E ≥ 120 J/mm³ (Page 6: 'Full densification threshold observed for Ti6Al4V is 120 J/mm³')
Measured at energy density E = 178 J/mm³ (defect-free condition)

Composition_Info.Note: Values from Table 3. Sum: 99.31% as reported in source

Process_Text.original: Ti6Al4V and Inconel 718 gas atomized powders (LPW, Runcorn, United Kingdom) were used throughout the work. IN718 powder size distribution was D10: 18 μm, D50: 30 μm, D90: 47 μm. Both powder feedstocks were spherical in shape. 3 mm thick IN718 plates were used for IN718 deposition. Layer thickness was fixed at 50 μm and the focal position of the laser beam (f) was fixed on the powder bed. Laser power, scan speed and hatch distance were varied. Fixed parameters: Layer thickness z (μm): 50, Focal position f (mm): 0, Process gas: Ar. Varied parameters: Hatch distance h (μm): 50-100, Laser power P (W): 250-350-450, Scan speed v (mm/s): 100-1000, Energy density E (J/mm³): 63-900. Optimal parameters for full densification: P = 350 W, v = 700 mm/s, h = 50 μm, z = 50 μm, E = 200 J/mm³.
Process_Text.simplified: Gas atomized IN718 powder (D50: 30 μm, spherical) processed via SLM on IN718 substrate in Ar atmosphere. Fixed: layer thickness 50 μm, focal position on powder bed. Varied: hatch 50-100 μm, laser power 250-450 W, scan speed 100-1000 mm/s, energy density 63-900 J/mm³. Optimal defect-free processing: P=350 W, v=700 mm/s, h=50 μm, z=50 μm, E=200 J/mm³.

Process_Info.Equipment: Powderful SLM prototype (nLIGHT alta 1 kW single mode fiber laser, 1080 nm wavelength, beam diameter 78 μm, Smart Move scanner head, 420 mm f-theta lens, LabVIEW control, Scan Master Designer software)

Microstructure_Text.original: The typical layer by layer SLM manufacturing characteristics, as well as the melt pool morphology are clearly visible. Columnar grains across several layers can also be distinguished, with a very fine dendritic structure. The SEM micrograph shows a detail of the dendritic structure of the material, with evidence of strong segregation in the interdendritic zones, which resulted enriched in Nb and Mo. The bright phases, observable in the BSE image, achieve Nb content up to 23.7 wt% (measured by EDX analysis) and Mo up to 2.5%.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Layer-by-layer SLM features with visible melt pool morphology. Columnar grains across multiple layers with fine dendritic structure. Strong segregation of Nb and Mo in interdendritic zones. Bright phases (δ-phase) with Nb up to 23.7 wt% and Mo up to 2.5 wt%.

Properties_Info.Note: Full densification threshold: E ≥ 250 J/mm³ (Page 6: 'Full densification threshold observed for IN718 is 250 J/mm³')
Measured at energy density E = 300 J/mm³ (defect-free condition, from Figure 9)

Process_Text.original: Four different premixed blends were used, with 10, 20, 30, and 40 wt% target IN718. Powders were mixed in the desired ratios with the innovative powder mixer unit of the open SLM platform. Fixed parameters: Layer thickness z (μm): 50, Hatch distance h (μm): 50, Focal position f (mm): 0, Process gas: Ar. Varied parameters: IN718 content (wt%): 10, Laser power P (W): 300-450, Scan speed v (mm/s): 350-850. For 10 wt% IN718 blend: P = 300 W, v = 700 mm/s, h = 50 μm, z = 50 μm, E = 170 J/mm³.
Process_Text.simplified: Pre-mixed blend of Ti6Al4V + 10 wt% IN718 processed via SLM. Fixed: layer thickness 50 μm, hatch 50 μm, focal position on powder bed, Ar atmosphere. Varied: laser power 300-450 W, scan speed 350-850 mm/s. Defect-free condition: P=300 W, v=700 mm/s, E=170 J/mm³.

Process_Info.Equipment: Powderful SLM prototype with double-hopper mixing unit (nLIGHT alta 1 kW single mode fiber laser, piezoelectric transducers for powder dosing, rotating blades for mixing, LabVIEW control)

Microstructure_Text.original: The SEM micrographs in Fig. 12a belonging to 10 wt% IN718 blend shows a non-uniform microstructure across the specimen surface. The deposited layers are clearly distinguishable and no alpha martensitic lathes could be observed. The alloying elements which compose IN718 (mainly Ni, Cr and Fe), act as beta-stabilizers for titanium, promoting the growth and retaining of this phase down to room temperature. The dendritic microstructure is not clearly observable within the layers, indicating a low microsegregation level. Instead, the presence of macrosegregated areas located at the layer interfaces is clearly evidenced. In these areas, Ni reaches values up to 15 wt% and Cr up to 3.6 wt%.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Non-uniform microstructure with clearly distinguishable deposited layers. No α' martensitic laths observed. IN718 alloying elements (Ni, Cr, Fe) act as β-stabilizers, promoting β phase retention at room temperature. Low microsegregation within layers. Macrosegregation at layer interfaces with Ni up to 15 wt% and Cr up to 3.6 wt%.

Properties_Info.Note: Measured at energy density E = 170 J/mm³ (defect-free condition). No cracks observed. No porosity detected (Page 7: 'for all the conditions no porosity was detected'). Main phase identification inferred from text description of β-stabilizing effect.

Process_Text.original: Four different premixed blends were used, with 10, 20, 30, and 40 wt% target IN718. Powders were mixed in the desired ratios with the innovative powder mixer unit of the open SLM platform. Fixed parameters: Layer thickness z (μm): 50, Hatch distance h (μm): 50, Focal position f (mm): 0, Process gas: Ar. Varied parameters: IN718 content (wt%): 20, Laser power P (W): 300-450, Scan speed v (mm/s): 350-850. For 20 wt% IN718 blend: P = 300 W, v = 450 mm/s, h = 50 μm, z = 50 μm, E = 267 J/mm³.
Process_Text.simplified: Pre-mixed blend of Ti6Al4V + 20 wt% IN718 processed via SLM. Fixed: layer thickness 50 μm, hatch 50 μm, focal position on powder bed, Ar atmosphere. Varied: laser power 300-450 W, scan speed 350-850 mm/s. Defect-free condition: P=300 W, v=450 mm/s, E=267 J/mm³ (crack-free above 266 J/mm³).

Process_Info.Equipment: Powderful SLM prototype with double-hopper mixing unit (nLIGHT alta 1 kW single mode fiber laser, piezoelectric transducers for powder dosing, rotating blades for mixing, LabVIEW control)

Microstructure_Text.original: In Fig. 12b the micrographs of the 20 wt% IN718 blend are shown. SEM analysis reveals a complex (yet uniform, as stated) microstructure, with clear evidence of a second phase nucleated in the interdendritic zones. The formation of β phase is expected with the increase of IN718 content. SEM/EDX analysis puts in to evidence that the second phase is rich in Ni (up to 17% wt).
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Complex yet uniform microstructure with clear evidence of second phase nucleated in interdendritic zones. β phase formation expected with increased IN718 content. Second phase rich in Ni (up to 17 wt%).

Properties_Info.Note: Measured at energy density E = 267 J/mm³ (defect-free condition). Cracks only observed below 266 J/mm³. Solidification range: 288°C (highest among blends). No porosity detected (Page 7). Main phase identification inferred from text.

Process_Text.original: Four different premixed blends were used, with 10, 20, 30, and 40 wt% target IN718. Powders were mixed in the desired ratios with the innovative powder mixer unit of the open SLM platform. Fixed parameters: Layer thickness z (μm): 50, Hatch distance h (μm): 50, Focal position f (mm): 0, Process gas: Ar. Varied parameters: IN718 content (wt%): 30, Laser power P (W): 300-450, Scan speed v (mm/s): 350-850. For 30 wt% IN718 blend: P = 300 W, v = 350 mm/s, h = 50 μm, z = 50 μm, E = 400 J/mm³. Specimens exhibited severe macro cracks regardless of process parameters.
Process_Text.simplified: Pre-mixed blend of Ti6Al4V + 30 wt% IN718 processed via SLM. Fixed: layer thickness 50 μm, hatch 50 μm, focal position on powder bed, Ar atmosphere. Varied: laser power 300-450 W, scan speed 350-850 mm/s. Parameters: P=300 W, v=350 mm/s, E=400 J/mm³. Severe macro cracks observed regardless of process parameters due to Ti₂Ni intermetallic formation.

Process_Info.Equipment: Powderful SLM prototype with double-hopper mixing unit (nLIGHT alta 1 kW single mode fiber laser, piezoelectric transducers for powder dosing, rotating blades for mixing, LabVIEW control)

Microstructure_Text.original: The further IN718 fraction increase, in the 30 wt% IN718 and 40 wt% IN718 blends, promotes the nucleation of the intermetallic Ti₂Ni phase (bright phase in Fig. 12c and d). It can be expected that the α-Ti + Ti₂Ni eutectoid transformation starts at grain boundaries of the β phase. Due to the massive precipitation of the intermetallic phase the dendritic solidification microstructure is no more observable. Thermodynamic calculations highlight an increase of Ti₂Ni formation at equilibrium temperature with increased IN718 fraction. For 30 wt% IN718 blends, Ti₂Ni starts to form in the liquid phase.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Promotes nucleation of intermetallic Ti₂Ni phase (bright phase). α-Ti + Ti₂Ni eutectoid transformation starts at β grain boundaries. Massive precipitation of intermetallic phase obscures dendritic solidification microstructure. Ti₂Ni forms in liquid phase (thermodynamic calculations).

Microstructure_Info.Main_Phase: β phase with α-Ti + Ti₂Ni eutectoid at grain boundaries

Properties_Info.Note: Measured at energy density E = 400 J/mm³. Severe macro cracks observed regardless of process parameters. Ti₂Ni forms in liquid phase causing cracking. Solidification range: 194°C. Porosity was not observed but density measurement prevented by cracking (Page 6: 'density was not measured since the presence of cracks hindered the image-based density calculation, however, porosity was not observed').

Composition_Info.Note: WARNING: Source Table 3 contains obvious data error for V (listed as 54.64, physically impossible). Corrected value of ~2.22 interpolated from trend of 10%, 20%, 30% blends. Sum with corrected V: 101.96% (rounded). Original Table 3 values: Ti: 56.87, Al: 3.13, V: 54.64 (ERROR), Ni: 20.00, Cr: 9.80, Fe: 7.00, Nb: 1.89, Mo: 1.05

Process_Text.original: Four different premixed blends were used, with 10, 20, 30, and 40 wt% target IN718. Powders were mixed in the desired ratios with the innovative powder mixer unit of the open SLM platform. Fixed parameters: Layer thickness z (μm): 50, Hatch distance h (μm): 50, Focal position f (mm): 0, Process gas: Ar. Varied parameters: IN718 content (wt%): 40, Laser power P (W): 300-450, Scan speed v (mm/s): 350-850. For 40 wt% IN718 blend: P = 300 W, v = 350 mm/s, h = 50 μm, z = 50 μm, E = 400 J/mm³. Specimens exhibited severe macro cracks regardless of process parameters.
Process_Text.simplified: Pre-mixed blend of Ti6Al4V + 40 wt% IN718 processed via SLM. Fixed: layer thickness 50 μm, hatch 50 μm, focal position on powder bed, Ar atmosphere. Varied: laser power 300-450 W, scan speed 350-850 mm/s. Parameters: P=300 W, v=350 mm/s, E=400 J/mm³. Severe macro cracks observed regardless of process parameters due to Ti₂Ni intermetallic formation.

Process_Info.Equipment: Powderful SLM prototype with double-hopper mixing unit (nLIGHT alta 1 kW single mode fiber laser, piezoelectric transducers for powder dosing, rotating blades for mixing, LabVIEW control)

Microstructure_Text.original: The further IN718 fraction increase, in the 30 wt% IN718 and 40 wt% IN718 blends, promotes the nucleation of the intermetallic Ti₂Ni phase (bright phase in Fig. 12c and d). It can be expected that the α-Ti + Ti₂Ni eutectoid transformation starts at grain boundaries of the β phase. Due to the massive precipitation of the intermetallic phase the dendritic solidification microstructure is no more observable. Thermodynamic calculations highlight an increase of Ti₂Ni formation at equilibrium temperature with increased IN718 fraction. For 40 wt% IN718 blends, Ti₂Ni starts to form in the liquid phase. The thermodynamic calculations predict the progressive destabilization of α-phase, with temperature formation decreased to values lower than 600 °C for the 40% blend.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Promotes nucleation of intermetallic Ti₂Ni phase (bright phase). α-Ti + Ti₂Ni eutectoid transformation starts at β grain boundaries. Massive precipitation of intermetallic phase obscures dendritic solidification microstructure. Ti₂Ni forms in liquid phase. α-phase destabilized with transformation temperature below 600°C.

Microstructure_Info.Main_Phase: β phase with α-Ti + Ti₂Ni eutectoid at grain boundaries

Properties_Info.Note: Measured at energy density E = 400 J/mm³. Severe macro cracks observed regardless of process parameters. Ti₂Ni forms in liquid phase causing cracking. Solidification range: 119°C. α-phase destabilized. Porosity was not observed but density measurement prevented by cracking (Page 6).

Composition_Info.Alloy_Name_Raw: Ti6Al4V to Ti6Al4V+20wt%IN718 functionally graded material
Composition_Info.Note: Graded composition from pure Ti6Al4V (0 wt% IN718) to Ti6Al4V + 20 wt% IN718. Build structure: 42 layers of pure Ti6Al4V (0% IN718), then 12 layers each at 5%, 10%, 15%, and 20% IN718. Total 90 layers. Measured composition shown is from Table 6 at height 3.62 mm, Region C (higher Ni content region). Full Table 6 data: 2.00 mm Region A (Ti: 84.63, Al: 6.11, V: 3.49, Cr: 0.28, Fe: 0.60, Ni: 0.98, Nb: 3.81), 2.00 mm Region B (Ti: 86.54, Al: 5.52, V: 3.39, Cr: 0.19, Fe: 0.32, Ni: 1.00, Nb: 3.03), 2.80 mm Region A (Ti: 75.79, Al: 4.89, V: 3.50, Cr: 3.15, Fe: 3.21, Ni: 6.32, Nb: 3.23), 2.80 mm Region B (Ti: 86.36, Al: 5.92, V: 4.16, Cr: 0.06, Fe: -, Ni: 0.16, Nb: 3.33), 3.62 mm Region A (Ti: 77.83, Al: 4.59, V: 3.48, Cr: 2.49, Fe: 2.49, Ni: 6.76, Nb: 2.80), 3.62 mm Region B (Ti: 86.11, Al: 5.64, V: 4.10, Cr: 0.21, Fe: 0.33, Ni: 0.94, Nb: 2.66), 3.62 mm Region C (Ti: 65.99, Al: 4.51, V: 2.35, Cr: 3.56, Fe: 4.70, Ni: 15.36, Nb: 3.53), 4.10 mm Region A (Ti: 79.04, Al: 4.70, V: 3.39, Cr: 2.12, Fe: 2.54, Ni: 6.91, Nb: 3.09), 4.10 mm Region B (Ti: 82.01, Al: 4.91, V: 3.68, Cr: 1.11, Fe: 1.74, Ni: 4.08, Nb: 2.46), 4.10 mm Region C (Ti: 67.77, Al: 3.90, V: 2.96, Cr: 2.24, Fe: 4.09, Ni: 15.68, Nb: 3.36). Ti decreases from ~85% to ~68%, Ni increases from ~1% to ~16% along build direction (Fig. 16).

Process_Text.original: The first material deposited was Ti6Al4V, for 42 layers. IN718 quantity was then increased in the following layers, in discrete steps of 5%wt, reaching 20% (for a total of four different blends). For each blend 12 layers of feedstock powder were deposited. Energy density determined according to the results of the premixed blend experiments, and was varied during the build according to the blend in use. Process parameters varied by layer: 0% IN718: P=300 W, v=700 mm/s, E=170 J/mm³, 42 layers. 5% IN718: P=300 W, v=700 mm/s, E=170 J/mm³, 12 layers. 10% IN718: P=300 W, v=700 mm/s, E=170 J/mm³, 12 layers. 15% IN718: P=300 W, v=550 mm/s, E=218 J/mm³, 12 layers. 20% IN718: P=300 W, v=450 mm/s, E=267 J/mm³, 12 layers. Fixed: h=50 μm, z=50 μm, f=0 mm, Ar atmosphere. The internal section appeared fully dense and crack free. No delamination between layers could be observed, nor between the first layers and the Ti6Al4V substrate.
Process_Text.simplified: Functionally graded specimen built on Ti6Al4V substrate. 42 layers pure Ti6Al4V (0% IN718), then 12 layers each at 5%, 10%, 15%, 20% IN718. Energy density varied in discrete steps: 170 J/mm³ (0-10% IN718), 218 J/mm³ (15% IN718), 267 J/mm³ (20% IN718). Fixed: hatch 50 μm, layer 50 μm, Ar. Parameters: P=300 W, v=450-700 mm/s. Fully dense, crack-free, no delamination. Laser remelting creates smooth transition between layers.

Process_Info.Equipment: Powderful SLM prototype with double-hopper mixing unit (nLIGHT alta 1 kW single mode fiber laser, piezoelectric transducers for powder dosing, rotating blades for mixing, LabVIEW control)

Microstructure_Text.original: The microstructure of the multigraded specimens taken at different build height positions. The variation of the chemical composition measurements along the build direction is shown in Fig. 16 (data has been acquired in points along the build direction which corresponds to the different Ti6Al4V/IN718 target compositions). The decrease in Ti content along the build direction, together with the increased presence of IN718 alloying elements, is coherent with the graded deposition. The dilution of the Ti6Al4V composition, due to the layer interdiffusion, is also observable by the corresponding Cr, Fe and Nb increase and the reduction of Al and V. The remelting effect induced by the laser beam (the previous solidified layers are melted together with the last deposited layer of powder) contributes in creating a smoother transition from one blend to the other, thus creating a whole new, functionally graded material.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Microstructure varies along build height corresponding to target compositions (Fig. 15). Ti content decreases, IN718 elements (Ni, Cr, Fe, Nb) increase along build direction (Fig. 16, Table 6). Layer interdiffusion and laser remelting create smooth transition between blends, forming functionally graded material. Highest hardness in layers enriched in Ni, Nb, Cr, Fe associated with hard intermetallic phases. Microstructure evolves from β-Ti (lower IN718) to complex α-Ti + Ti₂Ni eutectoid structures (higher IN718).

Microstructure_Info.Main_Phase: Functionally graded (varies from β-Ti to α-Ti + Ti₂Ni eutectoid structure along build direction)

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: Room temperature, Vickers microhardness profile along build height
Properties_Info.Note: Hardness increases smoothly from ~380 HV at base (0% IN718) to ~510 HV at top (20% IN718) along build height (Fig. 14). Smoother transition compared to premixed blends due to dilution effect from layer interdiffusion and laser remelting. Highest hardness in layers enriched in Ni, Nb, Cr, Fe associated with hard intermetallic phase formation. Local compositions measured by EDX (Table 6). Fully dense and crack-free specimen.
